introSection:
Lihpao Resort is a theme park located in Houli District, Taichung, Taiwan.

It currently has two roller coasters, including Gravity Max, the world's first coaster to feature a true 90-degree drop and the world's first tilt coaster. Besides Gravity Max, Lihpao Land has several themed areas with varied attractions, such as the Mine Express roller coaster, the Hook (a pirate ship ride), and the Wild Flume Adventure, a reversing log flume attraction.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The site where the theme park sits today used to be a ranch owned by Taiwan Sugar Corporation. The theme park was originally established in July 1998 as Yamay Recreation World or Yamay Discovery World. It was renamed to Lihpao Land in 2012.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.content:
* Discovery Walk
 * D Street
 * Enchanted Forest
 * Fairy Tale Village
 * Palace of Dreams
 * Yamay Island
 * Sky Dream - Taiwan's tallest ferris wheel, at 394 ft

sections.title: Transportation
sections.content: The theme park is accessible by bus from Fengyuan Station of Taiwan Railway.

sections.title: See also
sections.content: * List of tourist attractions in Taiwan

fullText:
Lihpao Resort is a theme park located in Houli District, Taichung, Taiwan.

It currently has two roller coasters, including Gravity Max, the world's first coaster to feature a true 90-degree drop and the world's first tilt coaster. Besides Gravity Max, Lihpao Land has several themed areas with varied attractions, such as the Mine Express roller coaster, the Hook (a pirate ship ride), and the Wild Flume Adventure, a reversing log flume attraction.

The site where the theme park sits today used to be a ranch owned by Taiwan Sugar Corporation. The theme park was originally established in July 1998 as Yamay Recreation World or Yamay Discovery World. It was renamed to Lihpao Land in 2012.


 * Discovery Walk
 * D Street
 * Enchanted Forest
 * Fairy Tale Village
 * Palace of Dreams
 * Yamay Island
 * Sky Dream - Taiwan's tallest ferris wheel, at 394 ft

The theme park is accessible by bus from Fengyuan Station of Taiwan Railway.
